method: Manual full-codebase review (Bugbot + Security subagents had no git diff on main)

bugbot.finding: submitForm accepts arbitrary answers with no server-side validation against the form definition (required fields, types).
Submission and lead inserts are not wrapped in a transaction; partial failure can leave orphaned rows.
getFormStats loads all submissions, leads, and analytics events via Drizzle with: — unbounded memory as forms grow.
slugify() can return an empty string for titles with only special characters.
Analytics useEffect fires form_viewed / form_started on pageIndex changes, inflating funnel metrics.
Multi-page forms do not validate required fields before advancing pages.
listLeads without formId fetches latest 200 leads globally then filters in memory — can miss older user leads.

security.finding: Public submitForm has no rate limiting or CAPTCHA — open to submission spam and DB flooding.
Public trackEvent has no rate limiting — easy to inflate analytics or bloat analytics_events.
BETTER_AUTH_SECRET is optional in env schema; misconfigured deploy could run without a proper secret.
No size limits on answers / metadata JSON blobs — large payloads enable storage abuse.
OpenAPI docs at /api are public — exposes full API surface if internet-facing.
Inngest handler is public; ensure INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY is set in production.

positive: Admin oRPC routes use authedContext with session checks
Form ownership verified on update, publish, and stats (createdBy)
Lead status updates check form ownership
Auth isolated in src/server/auth.ts (avoids pg leaking to client bundle)
Public forms only return published forms by slug
priority_fixes: Rate limiting on public submit and track endpoints
DB transaction for submission + lead creation
